75725.subject: Hyperliquid, Phantom Push CFTC on DeFi
75725.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

Here are the main news in web3:

* **[Hyperliquid and Phantom](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/hyperliquid-phantom-ask-cftc-to-exempt-defi-from-broker-rules)**[ ask the CFTC to exempt DeFi wallets from broker rules](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/hyperliquid-phantom-ask-cftc-to-exempt-defi-from-broker-rules)

* **[PayPal's PYUSD](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/paypal-s-pyusd-goes-native-on-polygon-joins-open-money-stack)**[ goes native on Polygon, joining its Open Money Stack](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/paypal-s-pyusd-goes-native-on-polygon-joins-open-money-stack)

* **[Aave Labs](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/aave-labs-launches-stable-vaults-for-fintech-stablecoin-yield)**[ launches Stable Vaults, turning variable rates into fixed fintech yield](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/aave-labs-launches-stable-vaults-for-fintech-stablecoin-yield)

* **[Interpol](https://thedefiant.io/news/security/interpol-ties-122-5m-crypto-wallet-to-romance-scam-ring)**[ ties a $122.5M crypto wallet to a romance-scam ring in a 97-country sweep](https://thedefiant.io/news/security/interpol-ties-122-5m-crypto-wallet-to-romance-scam-ring)

* **[Arbitrum](https://thedefiant.io/news/tokens/arbitrum-to-capture-10-of-fees-from-robinhood-chain)**[ will route 10% of Robinhood Chain's fees back to the ARB treasury](https://thedefiant.io/news/tokens/arbitrum-to-capture-10-of-fees-from-robinhood-chain)

* **[MGUSD, Explained: How MoneyGram Built a Stablecoin](https://thedefiant.io/education/explainers/mgusd-moneygram-stablecoin-stellar-distribution-explainer)**[ (Partner)](https://thedefiant.io/education/explainers/mgusd-moneygram-stablecoin-stellar-distribution-explainer)

**Hyperliquid**'s policy arm and wallet maker **Phantom** [filed a joint comment with the CFTC](https://thedefiant.io/news/regulation/hyperliquid-phantom-ask-cftc-to-exempt-defi-from-broker-rules) asking the agency to exempt non-custodial wallets and onchain infrastructure from exchange and broker registration. The filing would turn the **no-action relief** Phantom won in March into a formal rule covering every provider whose software never takes custody of user funds.

The ask matters more than any single exemption. A no-action letter shields one firm and can be pulled; a rule draws a line the whole sector can build behind. That two of DeFi's largest players are spending political capital to codify the non-custodial distinction signals a bet that the current CFTC will write it down — and a read that the window to do so is open now.

**Read more below!**

———————————————————————————

##### PARTNER

Caption: 

**Reach 110K+ crypto-native readers every weekday. **The Defiant lands in front of fund managers, founders, builders, and traders across the on

“The best way to predict the future is to invent it.”
_— Alan Kay _



----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/21d8e565-95c9-4b18-a24d-92b1cdb2d262/Screenshot_2026-07-09_at_16.47.53.png?t=1783630095)
Caption: 

# Thursday links: Crypto deal-making, futarchy, and predictable randomness

[The art of the crypto deal](https://www.wsj.com/business/deals/trump-backed-company-behind-family-crypto-wealth-in-talks-to-sell-core-business-59a2c1c5)

A _Wall Street Journal_ article on the looming collapse of a Nasdaq-listed crypto payments and digital asset treasury company — AI Financial — highlights the extraordinary economics of the Trump family’s crypto project, World Liberty Financial.

Formerly known as Alt5 Sigma, AI Financial recently “flagged risks about its survival” after reporting a quarterly loss of $271 million, primarily due to the decline of the World Liberty Financial governance token WLFI.

WLFI is AI Financial’s primary asset, acquired in two tranches: First, World Liberty received a controlling stake in AI Financial in return for $750 million worth of WLFI.

The company then raised money from investors to buy an additional $750 million of WLFI, directly from World Liberty.

As reported on President Trump's federal financial disclosure, the second transaction triggered a $540 million payment to the Trump family — because World Liberty's operating agreement entitles another Trump-controlled entity (DT Marks DEFI LLC) to 75% of the proceeds whenever World Liberty sells WLFI.

I believe this is unprecedented in financial history. 

How could it not be? Imagine Apple had an agreement that whenever it sold equity to investors, 75% of the proceeds went to the estate of Steve Jobs. 

Safe to say, it would never sell any equity: A new investor in Apple would have to immediately write down 75% of their investment.

Tokens aren’t equities, of course, so the analogy is not exact (there’s no reason to write-down the value of a governance token because they have no intrinsic value to begin with). 

The effect, however, appears to be the same. 

“The deals saddled AI Financial with an enormous pot of Trump cryptocurrency that since has slumped 70% in value,” _The Journal_ reports.

75723.subject: Crypto Biz: How stablecoins found their niche
75723.body:
Crypto Biz Newsletter

Автор: Cointelegraph


Автор: Cointelegraph

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/crypto-biz-how-stablecoins-found-niche-cointelegraph-updrc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B2TUd52dTTneOvl2jSurvVQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1BtOzNYMI5CIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mre0uil9~q8-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mre0uil9-q8
  
Crypto’s infrastructure is starting to look a lot more like traditional finance. New data from Dune shows that the world’s stablecoin leaders — Tether’s USDT and Circle’s USDC — are no longer competing for the same users, with each now dominating a different corner of the market. Meanwhile, demand for MiCA-compliant euro stablecoins is accelerating, hinting that the stablecoin economy is slowly expanding beyond the US dollar. Elsewhere in Crypto Biz, Strategy reignited debate over its “never sell” philosophy after offloading more than $200 million in Bitcoin (BTC) to fund shareholder dividends, while Vanguard signaled that even Wall Street’s biggest crypto skeptics are embracing tokenization. USDT, USDC use cases diverge as stablecoins become chain-specific USDT has become crypto’s dominant payments stablecoin while USDC has cemented itself as DeFi’s preferred settlement asset, according to new data from Dune. Rather than competing head-on, the industry’s two largest stablecoins are carving out distinct roles. USDT settled $95 billion in identified commercial payments during the first half of 2026 and continues to dominate business-to-business transfers. USDC, meanwhile, is driving onchain trading and DeFi activity, processing trillions of dollars in monthly transfer volume across Base and Ethereum.  The divergence suggests Tether and Circle are strengthening their positions where network effects are already on their side.  Continue Reading… Strategy sells more than $200 million in BTC Strategy sold 3,588 Bitcoin worth $216 million to fund preferred stock dividends, marking its largest sale since adopting BTC as its treasury asset. The sale trimmed Strategy’s holdings to 843,775 BTC and follows a new capital framework that allows Bitcoin sales to fund dividend payments. Even so, the company kept its $2.55 billion cash reserve intact, suggesting the biggest publicly traded BTC holder isn’t under liquidity pressure but is opting for greater financial flexibility as its preferred shares trade below par. The sale is unlikely to signal a broader shift away from Strategy’s Bitcoin accumulation strategy, according to Bernstein analysts. Still, it has fueled fresh debate over the company’s departure from co-founder Michael Saylor’s long-standing “never sell” mantra, even as Strategy remains the largest corporate buyer of Bitcoin.  Continue Re

75722.subject: Musk Says SpaceX Will Send ‘Thousands’ To The Moon And Mars In Next 10 Years
75722.body:
Forbes P.M.

Автор: Forbes


Автор: Forbes

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/musk-says-spacex-send-thousands-moon-mars-next-10-years-8qzvc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B73l3MhW4TSGJ2KEhWgwf%2FA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1XmHKpsL3PCsk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mre0geu9~s5-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mre0geu9-s5
  
Good evening, SpaceX CEO Elon Musk predicted SpaceX would send humans back to the moon and Mars in the next five years and build permanent cities on the lunar surface in the next 10 years. He also said the company’s eventual goal was to build a “full-blown, self-sustaining city on the moon, like an actual metropolis.” Here’s what to know about his claims. Thanks for reading! Kris Kornbluth Assistant Editor, Platforms & Curation More Recommended Reads Saul Loeb/AFP via Getty Images Trump's Qatari Jet Secure From 'Enemies Of America', White House Says—But Questions Remain Steven Cheung, White House communications director, told Forbes the jet has been “fitted with high-level security protocols that ensure the safety of the President and his staff.” Getty Images OpenAI And Anthropic Are Preparing For IPOs—Here’s How They Stack Up OpenAI and Anthropic are both facing difficult market conditions, and whichever one goes public first is likely to determine the trajectory of the one that follows.
Приєднатися до обговорення

AI-curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: CryptoBeholder.com


Автор: CryptoBeholder.com

Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-july-9-cryptobeholder-aewzf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3Bkcs3DYHwQeCFoaQ3Vj%2B0GQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0QS_--b--ZCck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrdps80f~qv-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrdps80f-qv
  
🧠 AI's Crypto News Brief: Infrastructure and institutions took center stage as Swift and Sony pushed deeper into tokenization, while a wallet exploit, a $122M scam bust, and a stalled market-structure bill kept sentiment cautious. Adoption advances, but safety and regulatory questions linger. 🏦 Swift activates blockchain ledger with 17 global banks Swift switched on its blockchain-based shared ledger for initial use, with 17 major banks — including HSBC, UBS, Wells Fargo and Citi — preparing to pilot live 24/7 cross-border payments using tokenized deposits, a significant step toward mainstream tokenization. Read full story → 🏛️ Sony Bank wins US regulatory nod to issue stablecoins Sony Bank secured conditional approval from the OCC to establish a US stablecoin trust bank, capitalized with $40 million, marking another major corporate entrant into the regulated dollar-stablecoin market. More details → 🪙 New Hampshire becomes first state to approve Bitcoin treasury New Hampshire became the first US state to approve Bitcoin treasury investments and is now eyeing a $100 million Bitcoin-backed bond, a landmark move that could set a template for state-level crypto adoption. Read more → 🚨 Interpol busts $122M crypto romance-scam network An Interpol operation uncovered a single crypto wallet that processed more than $122.5 million tied to romance-scam laundering, part of a global anti-fraud sweep that led to 5,811 arrests. See details → 🔓 Cardano wallet exploit drains $2.4M in ADA A flaw in SecondFi's wallet address-generation system drained roughly $2.4 million in ADA from 374 wallets, prompting EMURGO to step back from Cardano's Pentad infrastructure group to focus on fund recovery. Read full story → 📉 Strategy sells 3,588 BTC in its largest divestment yet Strategy offloaded 3,588 BTC in its biggest Bitcoin sale since going all-in on the asset, a move that could reshape how corporate treasuries manage holdings and signals a notable shift in sentiment. More details → ⚖️ CLARITY Act misses July deadline as August 7 looms Congress missed its self-imposed July 4 deadline to pass the CLARITY Act, and the crypto market-structure bill now faces roughly a 50/50 shot of clearing the Senate before an August 7 target. Read more → 📬 Subscribe for the AI-curated daily crypto digest at cryptobeholder.com
Продовжити читати на LinkedIn
